truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 8 of 20 true vulnerabilities (40% recall). Of the 8 findings, 5 matched true vulnerabilities with full fidelity, 1 matched partially (audit-logging command injection maps to SC-V3-013 insecure temp file handling), and 2 findings had severity or CWE misclassifications. 13 true vulnerabilities were completely missed, including a critical use-after-free (SC-V3-003), a critical integer underflow (SC-V3-016), a logic bomb (SC-V3-007), and a stack buffer overflow (SC-V3-005) that was partially covered by finding 6 but not via the safe_string_copy angle. No Run 2 findings were false positives — all 8 were genuinely vulnerable code, though some were under-scoped or miscategorized.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Format String Vulnerability in Debug Logger", "function_or_symbol": "string_utils::log_debug_message", "evidence": "std::string active_format = runtime_log_format(); printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding 4 matches exactly: runtime_log_format() returns a config-driven format string passed as the format specifier to printf with user_input as the argument. Same function, same mechanism, same evidence.

Run 2 never mentions the factorial function (fact) or integer overflow in it. The fact lambda at lines 312-320 uses long long result with result *= i, which overflows for inputs >= 21, but Run 2's findings list contains no reference to factorial or integer overflow.

Run 2 never discusses stage_deferred_history_flush, the deferred task lambda capturing scratch pointers, or g_memory_pool.cleanup() invalidating those pointers. The use-after-free in the deferred flush pipeline is entirely absent from the audited answer.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Command Injection via Config Validation", "function_or_symbol": "ConfigLoader::validate_config", "evidence": "std::string command = validator_cmd + \" \" + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding 2 matches exactly: validate_config concatenates config-controlled strings and passes them to system(). Same function, same vulnerability, same evidence.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression", "function_or_symbol": "SuperCalc::evaluate_expression", "evidence": "if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }"
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding 6 catches the strcpy overflow in evaluate_expression, which is one anchor of SC-V3-005. However, the true vulnerability also encompasses the safe_string_copy function itself (dest[src_len]; src_len > dest_size anchors), which Run 2 never analyzes. The safe_string_copy truncation logic is actually safe, but the true vulnerability description groups the stack buffer overflow broadly across input processing — Run 2 only covers the evaluate_expression half.

Run 2 never discusses ThreadSafeCounter, its volatile long long value_, or the classic load-before-lock race (old_value read outside the lock_guard). The concurrency flaw in increment() is entirely absent.

Run 2 never mentions the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE logic bomb, login_attempts_ > 5 backdoor, or the bypass of the admin secret after 6 failed attempts. The authenticate function's subverted fallback is completely unanalyzed.
Run 2 never discusses config_dir_ + filename path concatenation, directory traversal via ../ sequences in load_config, or the admin exec load command path. Path traversal is entirely absent.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser", "function_or_symbol": "ExpressionParser::parse", "evidence": "buffer_size_ = 256; ... strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding 5 matches exactly: parse() builds expr_str from concatenated token texts and strcpy's it into the 256-byte pool-allocated expression_buffer_. Same function, same overflow, same evidence.
Run 2 finding 7 matches exactly: the admin secret is a constexpr char array in the source, extractable from the binary. Same constant, same evidence.
Run 2 never mentions generate_session_token, std::mt19937, the 4-digit token space (1000-9999), or the predictability of session tokens. Weak PRNG is entirely absent.

Run 2 never discusses log_debug_message's static std::tm cached_time, the localtime() call on a static object, or the data race between threads reading/writing cached_time. Thread-unsafe logging is entirely absent.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Command Injection in Audit Event Logging", "function_or_symbol": "AdminConsole::append_override_audit_event", "evidence": "std::string command = \"sh -c \\\"echo Admin override logged >> \\" + audit_path + \\\"\\\"\"; system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding 3 catches the command injection in append_override_audit_event, which is the same code path as SC-V3-013. However, Run 2 characterizes it as command injection (CWE-78) rather than insecure temp file handling (CWE-377). The core issue — constructing a temp path from an attacker-controllable environment variable (TMPDIR/TEMP) — is not articulated in the finding. The command injection is a consequence of the insecure temp file handling, so the finding is partial.
Run 2 never discusses the persistent authenticated_ flag, session_token_, or login_attempts_ surviving across sessions — the session never expires or resets. Persistent auth state is entirely absent.

Run 2 never mentions handle_variable_command, the std::stod(tokens[3]) call, or the std::invalid_argument/std::out_of_range exceptions it can throw unhandled. Variable parsing crash is entirely absent.

Run 2 never discusses MemoryPool::allocate's block splitting logic, SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES, or the integer underflow in previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES when the block is only slightly larger than the request. The underflow producing a massive fragment.size is entirely absent.

Run 2 never discusses parse_expression/parse_term/parse_factor recursion, the MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH constant, or stack exhaustion from deeply nested parenthesized expressions. Unbounded recursion is entirely absent.

Run 2 never discusses ExpressionEvaluator::result_cache_, evaluate_cached, or the fact that schedule_expression_analysis enqueues cache updates on pool threads while the main thread may read the cache simultaneously — a data race on the unordered_map with no lock in evaluate_cached. Data race in cache is entirely absent.

Run 2 never discusses load_config's filesystem::exists check followed by sleep_for(25ms) then ifstream open — a classic TOCTOU window where the file can be swapped between existence check and read. TOCTOU is entirely absent.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "title": "Division by Zero in Route Scorer", "function_or_symbol": "SuperCalc::calculate_route_score", "evidence": "return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);"
Items.Notes: Run 2 finding 8 matches exactly: calculate_route_score divides by token_length which is 0 for empty input. Same function, same division, same evidence.
